title: Flash Attention (FMHA) ASM Optimization Patterns - CK on gfx942
source: 25 forward + 156 backward disassembled kernels from aiter/3rdparty/composable_kernel

forward_pass.description: Computes S=Q*K^T (scaled), P=softmax(S), O=P*V. Single fused kernel per head-dim variant.

architecture.tile_size: 32x32 BF16 (larger tiles for forward, fewer MFMAs but more FP per MFMA)
architecture.accumulation: FP32 in VGPRs v[64:79] (16 registers per 32x32 tile output)

memory_strategy.q_loading: Pre-loaded into VGPRs before main loop
memory_strategy.k_loading: Direct-to-LDS via buffer_load_dword ... lds, then ds_read_b128 into VGPRs
memory_strategy.v_loading: Direct global loads into VGPRs (buffer_load_dword v224-v227) during Q*K^T MFMA chain
memory_strategy.direct_to_lds_count: 4 buffer_load_dword lds per K-tile iteration (m0 stepped by 0x880)
memory_strategy.software_pipelining: Next-iteration V loads interleaved with current Q*K^T MFMAs

main_loop_pattern.description: Per K-tile iteration: ds_read K from LDS -> barrier -> Q*K^T MFMA chain (16 MFMAs) with interleaved V loads -> P*V MFMAs
interleaving_detail: ds_read_b128 x8 (read K tile from LDS into VGPRs v[160:223])
v_mfma Q*K -> buffer_load_dword lds (next K tile, 1 per 2 MFMAs)
v_mfma Q*K -> buffer_load_dword V (next iteration global loads)
main_loop_pattern.mfma_to_load_ratio: 2 MFMAs per 1 buffer_load (optimal for hiding VMEM latency)

epilogue.causal_masking: v_cndmask_b32 to zero out future positions (per-element mask based on position comparison)
epilogue.softmax: Online softmax using running max and rescaling, no DPP needed (per-lane computation within MFMA tiles)

variants.rtna: Round to nearest away (BF16 conversion mode)
variants.rtne: Round to nearest even (adds ~700 instructions for rounding logic)
variants.causal: Adds causal mask check (~500 more instructions)
variants.group: Group Query Attention (extra indexing for shared KV heads, ~20 more instructions)

backward_pass.description: Computes dQ, dK, dV from dO. Much more complex than forward: requires recomputing S, P, and computing multiple gradient matrices.

architecture.tile_size: 16x16 BF16 (smaller tiles than forward for finer-grained gradient computation)
architecture.accumulation: FP32 in VGPRs v[48:55] for dQ, dK, dV partial results; AGPRs a[0:255] for MFMA operands

register_usage.note: Full AGPR utilization (a[0:255]) - single wave occupancy, all 512 GPRs used

dpp_patterns.purpose: Softmax gradient computation: dS_ij = P_ij * (dP_ij - sum_j(P_ij * dP_ij))

quad_broadcast.description: Broadcast lane N within each 4-lane quad to all 4 lanes. Used for distributing the row-sum (D_i = sum_j P_ij*dO_ij) to all elements in that quad for computing dS = P*(dP - D).
code_sequence: // Step 1: Broadcast D_i (row-sum) to each quad element
v_mov_b32_dpp v127, v124 quad_perm:[3,3,3,3]  // D for lane 3 -> all
v_mov_b32_dpp v126, v124 quad_perm:[2,2,2,2]  // D for lane 2 -> all
v_mov_b32_dpp v125, v124 quad_perm:[1,1,1,1]  // D for lane 1 -> all
v_mov_b32_dpp v124, v124 quad_perm:[0,0,0,0]  // D for lane 0 -> all
// Step 2: Compute dS = P * (dP - D) via fused subtract-broadcast
v_subrev_f32_dpp v72, v150, v72 quad_perm:[0,0,0,0]  // v72 -= D[lane0]
v_subrev_f32_dpp v73, v150, v73 quad_perm:[1,1,1,1]  // v73 -= D[lane1]
v_subrev_f32_dpp v74, v150, v74 quad_perm:[2,2,2,2]  // v74 -= D[lane2]
v_subrev_f32_dpp v75, v150, v75 quad_perm:[3,3,3,3]  // v75 -= D[lane3]

quad_swap.description: Swap adjacent pairs within each quad. Used for BF16 packing / data layout transformation between MFMA stages.
quad_swap.code_example: v_mov_b32_dpp v18, v72 quad_perm:[1,0,3,2]  // swap pairs for packing

dpp_patterns.cycle_savings_vs_lds: Without DPP, the D_i broadcast would require ds_write + s_barrier + ds_read per quad (~40 cycles). With DPP: 4 cycles for broadcast + 8 cycles for fused subtract = 12 cycles. Savings: ~28 cycles per iteration, repeated 2x per K-tile = ~56 cycles saved per tile.

memory_strategy.q_k_v_loading: buffer_load_dword idxen (indexed global loads) for Q, K, V tiles
memory_strategy.gradient_accumulation: ds_write_b32 for partial dQ/dK/dV to LDS, then ds_read_b128 for reduction
memory_strategy.lse_loading: Pre-loaded log-sum-exp from forward pass for numerically stable softmax backward

software_pipelining.vmcnt_pattern: vmcnt(32) lgkmcnt(0) before barriers - 32+ loads in flight
software_pipelining.lgkmcnt_pattern: lgkmcnt(4) for precise LDS read tracking
software_pipelining.interleaving: MFMA -> ds_write partial gradient -> MFMA -> ds_read for accumulation -> MFMA

phases_per_ktile: Phase 1: Load K tile, compute S = Q*K^T (16 MFMAs with ds_write interleaved)
Phase 2: DPP softmax gradient: broadcast D, compute dS = P*(dP-D)
Phase 3: v_perm_b32 for BF16 packing of dS elements
Phase 4: Compute dQ += dS * K (16 MFMAs with ds_read interleaved)
Phase 5: Compute dK += dS^T * Q (16 MFMAs)
Phase 6: Compute dV += P^T * dO (16 MFMAs)
main_loop_structure.mfma_per_phase: ~80 MFMAs per K-tile (5-6 phases x 16 MFMAs each)

variants.pssk: Split-K for dS computation (distributes K-tiles across workgroups, atomic add)
variants.pddv: Split-K for dQ, dK, dV accumulation (extra global atomic adds)
variants.psskddv: Both split-K modes enabled (most complex, ~6.6K instructions)
variants.a16: FP16 atomics for gradient accumulation (faster but less precise)

1_mfma_interleaving.description: Interleave MFMA with memory operations to hide latency
1_mfma_interleaving.forward: 2 MFMAs per 1 buffer_load (K to LDS, V to VGPR)
1_mfma_interleaving.backward: MFMA -> ds_write -> MFMA -> ds_read -> MFMA (3-way interleave)

2_dpp_softmax_gradient.description: Use DPP quad_perm to broadcast row-sums within 4-lane quads, avoiding LDS
2_dpp_softmax_gradient.applicable_to: Any kernel computing softmax gradients or per-row reductions that map to quads

3_direct_to_lds_loading.description: buffer_load_dword ... lds bypasses VGPRs, reduces register pressure
3_direct_to_lds_loading.forward_usage: 4 direct-to-LDS loads per K-tile (via m0 address register stepping)

4_full_agpr_utilization.description: Backward uses all 256 AGPRs for MFMA operand storage, freeing VGPRs for indexing/control
4_full_agpr_utilization.trade_off: 1 wave occupancy but maximum compute throughput

5_precise_waitcnt.description: Use vmcnt(N) with N>0 to allow partial wait instead of full drain
5_precise_waitcnt.forward: s_waitcnt lgkmcnt(0) before barriers (full LDS drain)
5_precise_waitcnt.backward: s_waitcnt vmcnt(32) lgkmcnt(0) - 32 loads still in flight while processing

6_v_perm_b32_packing.description: v_perm_b32 for BF16/FP16 byte rearrangement between compute stages
6_v_perm_b32_packing.usage: Pack two FP32 results into BF16 pair for next MFMA input

rmsnorm_kernels_cu.what_it_uses: hipcub::BlockReduce for variance sum (generic, not DPP-optimized)
rmsnorm_kernels_cu.what_it_should_use: DPP butterfly reduction (like layernorm ASM: row_ror:8,4,2,1) via hip_reduce.h block_reduce

fused_qk_norm_rope_cache_quant_cu.what_it_uses: __shfl_xor with width=32 (CUDA-style, ignores half the AMD wavefront)
fused_qk_norm_rope_cache_quant_cu.what_it_should_use: wave_reduce from hip_reduce.h (DPP-based, uses full 64-lane wavefront)
